architecture.summary: The paper proposes an attention-based resonator network for decomposing bound hypervectors in Vector Symbolic Architectures (VSAs). The system iteratively factorizes a composite vector (formed by element-wise binding of attribute vectors) into its constituent factors by replacing the traditional Hopfield update rule with a self-attention-inspired update based on a log-sum-exp energy function with norm-bounded states. Information flows through iterative application of the attention-based update rule, which applies similarity-weighted linear combinations of codebook vectors to progressively refine estimates of each factor until convergence.

biblio.title: Self-Attention Based Semantic Decomposition in Vector Symbolic Architectures

claims.statement: The attention-based resonator network enables use of the resonator network with continuous factors rather than bipolar factors
The attention-based resonator network outperforms the traditional resonator network for multiple factors (k ≥ 3), with an increasing gap in performance as k increases
The attention-based update rule requires significantly fewer iterations to converge compared to the traditional resonator network

baseline.name: original resonator network adapted for FHRR

claims.statement: For FHRR hypervectors, the adapted original resonator network significantly underperforms compared to the attention-based resonator network

claims.dataset: synthetic bundled factorization with Gaussian noise
claims.metric: success rate (probability of correct factorization convergence)
claims.statement: For noise-robustness with bipolar vectors and D=10000, the attention resonator network succeeds with probability 0.97 while the original succeeds with probability 0.08
The original FHRR-adapted resonator network has near-zero accuracy across all parameter configurations tested

The attention-based FHRR and attention-based bipolar resonator networks achieve an order of magnitude lower complexity (average steps for correct convergence) compared to the original bipolar resonator network
The resulting network has high robustness against error and can store exponentially many combinations of codebook factors
